api: Dremio REST API v3 (https://docs.dremio.com/software/rest-api/, redirects to https://docs.dremio.com/current/reference/api/)
scope: Pass B full-surface expansion, re-researched against the live docs tree (docs.dremio.com/current/reference/api/{catalog,reflections,source,user,roles,job,sql,personal-access-token,engine-management,search,scripts,wlm}). All paths below are relative to config.base_url exactly as legacy/this bundle already resolves it (the /api/v3 or Cloud /v0 version segment lives inside base_url, not in these declared paths) — this preserves the 4 pre-existing parity-locked streams' exact wire paths (catalog/reflections/source/user, including legacy's own plural 'reflections' naming) unchanged. New in this pass: the roles read stream, and 10 write actions covering user/role/reflection/personal-access-token lifecycle mutation (the real API's singular 'reflection'/'role' path segments are used for these NEW writes, matching the documented v3 shape exactly, since they are not constrained by any pre-existing parity-locked path). Excluded: arbitrary SQL job submission (code-execution capability, not a record mutation), job/search/script/engine/workload-management/external-token-provider/LDAP-cache surfaces (admin/operational tooling with no natural record-list or record-mutation shape this dialect targets), and the AWS-Glue-specific permission-cache endpoint (narrow, source-type-gated operational action).

excluded.reason: single catalog-entry detail; identical record shape to the catalog list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
catalog-entry lookup by path instead of id; same record shape as the catalog list stream

creates a new catalog object (source/space/folder/dataset) with type-specific config payloads (connection credentials for sources, SQL for views); the shape varies per catalog object type and cannot be expressed as one uniform writes.json action without a type-dispatch the dialect has no primitive for

updates a catalog object's type-specific config (e.g. a source's connection credentials); same type-dispatch limitation as create, and risks storing/echoing source connection secrets through a generic record body

deletes an arbitrary catalog object (source/space/folder/table/view); a single generic delete-by-id here could remove an entire configured source and every dataset beneath it, a much larger blast radius than the other modeled deletes
forces catalog metadata refresh for a dataset/source; an operational cache-invalidation action, not a syncable record mutation
dataset lineage graph; a derived relationship view, not a syncable object list
catalog-object tag sub-resource; narrow per-object metadata with no natural top-level list endpoint to enumerate across the catalog
sets tags on one catalog object at a time; narrow per-object metadata mutation, not a general syncable-collection write
catalog-object wiki/description sub-resource; narrow per-object metadata with no top-level list endpoint
sets the wiki/description on one catalog object at a time; narrow per-object metadata mutation
single-reflection detail; identical record shape to the reflections list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
reflections for one dataset, filtered view of the same records the reflections stream already reads in full

creates a new reflection with type-specific field configuration (dimensionFields/measureFields for aggregation, displayFields for raw); the shape is dataset-schema-dependent and not a fixed record layout this dialect can express as one action
single-source detail; identical record shape to the sources list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
clears the AWS Lake Formation permission cache; narrow, source-type-gated (AWS Glue Data Catalog sources only) operational cache-invalidation action, not a data mutation
single-user detail; identical record shape to the users list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
user lookup by name instead of id; same record shape as the users list stream

lists a user's Personal Access Token metadata; enumerating PAT labels/ids per user requires a user-id fan-out this dialect can express for writes but not as a first-class read stream without an id-source config the operator would have to hand-maintain
revokes every PAT belonging to one user in a single call, immediately invalidating all of that user's API clients; broader blast radius than the single-token delete this bundle models
revokes every Personal Access Token in the entire Dremio instance; system-wide credential invalidation, far outside a reverse-ETL write's intended scope
single-role detail; identical record shape to the roles list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
role lookup by name instead of id; same record shape as the roles list stream
adds/removes role members via a diff-shaped add/remove list body, not a single-record create/update/delete this dialect's uniform write shapes target

submits arbitrary caller-supplied SQL for execution on the Dremio cluster; an arbitrary-code-execution capability (can run DDL/DML against any accessible source depending on grants), not a bounded record mutation — legacy never implemented this either
job status/metadata lookup for a job id obtained from a SQL submission this bundle does not implement; no reachable id source without submit_sql
paginated SQL result-set rows for a job id obtained from a SQL submission this bundle does not implement; no reachable id source without submit_sql
cancels a running SQL job; unreachable without submit_sql, which is itself excluded as requires_elevated_scope
saved-SQL-script management; operational SQL-authoring tooling, not a syncable data object relevant to a reverse-ETL/read connector
creates a saved SQL script; SQL-authoring tooling, not a data mutation
single saved-script detail; same scope narrowing as the scripts list
updates a saved SQL script; SQL-authoring tooling
deletes a saved SQL script; SQL-authoring tooling
script-level privilege grants; narrow admin metadata on an already-excluded resource
updates script-level privilege grants; narrow admin metadata on an already-excluded resource
full-text/entity search across tables, views, reflections, jobs, functions, scripts, and folders; a query-shaped operation, not a syncable list-all stream
search-index health/status snapshot, not a syncable object stream
triggers removal of every indexed search document/artifact and an async cluster reindex
Dremio Cloud-specific compute-engine management; not part of Dremio Software's surface, and this bundle's flat unscoped paths target the Software/legacy-compatible shape
Dremio Cloud-specific compute-engine creation; not part of Dremio Software's surface
Dremio Cloud-specific compute-engine lifecycle control; not part of Dremio Software's surface
Dremio Cloud-specific compute-engine lifecycle control; not part of Dremio Software's surface
Dremio Cloud-specific compute-engine deletion; not part of Dremio Software's surface
workload-management queue configuration; cluster-tuning admin surface, not a syncable data object
workload-management rule configuration; cluster-tuning admin surface, not a syncable data object
external identity-token-provider configuration for SSO integration; identity-provider credential surface, not syncable data
LDAP authorization cache control, an operational admin toggle rather than a data object
